phases.label: Candidate Nomination
phases.description: Candidates are nominated by members of State Legislative Assemblies.
voterActions: Citizens do not vote directly; follow your MLAs' choices
Understand the party strength in your State Assembly

phases.label: Indirect Polling
phases.description: MLAs vote using the Single Transferable Vote (STV) system.
steps: Open ballot system (MLAs show vote to party agent)
voterActions: Monitor the results as they represent your state's interests
Follow the 'Open Ballot' rules for transparency

glossary.definition: The 'Council of States' or Upper House of the Indian Parliament. It is a permanent body and not subject to dissolution.
An election where voters do not cast ballots directly but elect representatives (MLAs) who then vote for the candidates.
A proportional representation system where voters rank candidates and votes are transferred based on surplus or elimination.
Elections held every two years to fill the seats of one-third of the members who retire.
12 members nominated by the President of India for their contributions to art, literature, science, or social service.
The Vice-President of India, who presides over the Rajya Sabha sessions.
A system where MLAs must show their marked ballot paper to their party's authorized agent before dropping it in the box.
The minimum number of votes required for a candidate to be declared elected in the STV system.
Members of Rajya Sabha are elected for a term of 6 years, with one-third retiring every second year.
The body whose members (MLAs) elect the representatives for the Rajya Sabha.
Seats are allocated to each state/territory based on population. Fourth Schedule of the Constitution deals with this.
When an MLA votes against their party's candidate. In Rajya Sabha, this usually leads to disqualification under the Anti-Defection Law.
A bill that can only be introduced in Lok Sabha. Rajya Sabha has limited powers — it can only suggest changes within 14 days.
A meeting of both houses to resolve deadlocks on ordinary bills. Does not apply to Money Bills or Constitutional Amendments.
A cabinet minister (often the PM if they are a member of RS) who leads the government's business in the Rajya Sabha.
The time following Question Hour where members can raise matters of urgent public importance without prior notice.
Permanent committees that scrutinize government spending and legislation in detail.
Legal immunities granted to members to ensure they can perform their duties without fear of prosecution for things said in the House.

quickQuestions: Can I vote in Rajya Sabha elections?
How long is a Rajya Sabha term?
Who is the Chairman of Rajya Sabha?

quiz.question: What is the term length of a Member of the Rajya Sabha?
quiz.explanation: While the Rajya Sabha itself is a permanent body, individual members are elected for a 6-year term.
quiz.funFact: One-third of the members retire every two years, ensuring continuity.

quiz.question: Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
quiz.explanation: The Vice-President of India automatically becomes the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
quiz.funFact: If the Vice-President acts as President, they do not perform the duties of the RS Chairman.

quiz.question: How many members are nominated by the President to the Rajya Sabha?
quiz.explanation: The President nominates 12 members with special knowledge or practical experience in fields like Science, Art, and Literature.
quiz.funFact: Famous nominees have included Sachin Tendulkar, Rekha, and Mary Kom.

quiz.question: Which system is used for electing members to the Rajya Sabha?
quiz.explanation: Rajya Sabha members are elected by the elected members of State Legislative Assemblies using STV with proportional representation.
quiz.funFact: This ensures that parties are represented in the Rajya Sabha in proportion to their strength in the state assemblies.

quiz.question: What is the minimum age to be a member of the Rajya Sabha?
quiz.explanation: A candidate must be at least 30 years old. For Lok Sabha, the minimum is 25.
quiz.funFact: The Rajya Sabha is often called the 'House of Elders' because of this higher age requirement.

quiz.question: Within how many days must the Rajya Sabha return a Money Bill to the Lok Sabha?
quiz.explanation: The Rajya Sabha can only delay a Money Bill for 14 days. If it doesn't return it, the bill is deemed passed by both houses.
quiz.funFact: The Rajya Sabha cannot reject or amend a Money Bill; it can only make recommendations that the Lok Sabha may or may not accept.
